title: Autosuficiență locală vs. sistem comunist: calea termoficării moderne
summary: The article analyzes Bucharest's district heating crisis, where over 3,500 buildings lost adequate heating after a major plant failure in January 2026. It critiques simplistic solutions like individual electric heating, arguing instead for EU-funded modernization, energy efficiency improvements, and decentralized thermal islands inspired by European capitals like Copenhagen, Berlin, Amsterdam, and Vienna.
talking_points: Over 3,500 buildings in Bucharest lost adequate heating after the CET Sud breakdown, affecting 37-38% of connected buildings
Individual electric heating proposed by Prof. Cristian Păun would collapse the electrical grid and ignores cogeneration realities
European capitals like Copenhagen, Berlin, Amsterdam, Vienna, and Oslo are modernizing their district heating systems using local resources, waste-to-energy, geothermal, and heat pumps
Mayor Ciprian Ciucu presented a project for 24 modular mini thermal plants near deficit points, originally started in 2022
The EU LIFE 22 CETHEAT project involving Termoenergetica aims to create a new thermo-energy strategy for Bucharest by September
author: Cosmin Gabriel Păcuraru
